 Amazon, gig companies see spike in workers on SNAP and Medicaid, study shows 
 
 Amazon workers on federal aid nearly tripled between 2020 and 2025, while ride-hailing and food delivery app drivers shot up on lists compiled by the U.S. Government Accountability Office.

Since 2020, Walmart spent $37.6 billion in stock buybacks—enough to give all their workers a $23,550 bonus. 
 
Instead, Walmart pays wages so low that many of their workers need Medicaid & SNAP paid for by taxpayers. 
 
The real welfare queen is the Walton family worth $485 billion.
